checklist.question: Did receivables grow materially faster than revenue across consecutive periods?
Did non-current 'soft' assets (intangibles, other assets, capitalized items) grow materially faster than the business overall?

evidence.quote: IntangibleAssetsNetExcludingGoodwill=100,875,503.14 (2011-01-29) declined to 97,674,748.41 (2012-01-28) and roughly stable at 99,303,908.81 (2013-02-02)
Goodwill=5,552,912.95 held constant across all reported periods (2011-01-29 through 2013-08-03)

checklist.question: Is there a persistent divergence between net income and operating cash flow?

evidence.quote: Q1 FY2013: NetIncomeLoss=4,556,979.45 (2013-02-03 to 2013-05-04) vs NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivities=-5,734,870.08 (same period)
FY2012 NetIncomeLoss=5,958,290.89 vs NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivities=30,989,473.06 (2012-01-29 to 2013-02-02) - large positive reversal after prior year near-zero CFO

checklist.question: Do inventory levels or gross margins show trends inconsistent with revenue?

evidence.quote: GrossProfit/Revenue margin compressed from ~36-37% (FY2010: GrossProfit=113,706,701.29 / SalesRevenueNet=307,509,159.93) to ~33-34% (FY2011: GrossProfit=130,325,586.68 / SalesRevenueNet=384,665,826.57), then stabilized in FY2012 (GrossProfit=127,692,441.57 / SalesRevenueNet=379,393,095.40)

checklist.question: Is the earnings trajectory unusually smooth relative to the volatility of revenue and cash flow?

evidence.quote: Quarterly NetIncomeLoss swings widely and turns negative in weak quarters: -983,051.57 (2012-04-29 to 2012-07-28) and -1,139,244.86 (2013-05-05 to 2013-08-03), tracking revenue seasonality (SalesRevenueNet quarterly ranges from ~62.6M to ~113.8M)

checklist.question: Did liability, allowance, or reserve balances decline (or fail to grow) despite growing business activity?

evidence.quote: AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent=11,171,845.73 (2012-01-28) declined to 10,403,358.65 (2013-02-02) and further to 8,046,772.28 (2013-08-03), while AccountsReceivableNetCurrent grew from 58,597,844.50 (2012-01-28) to 70,240,282.90 (2013-02-02)

evidence.location: derived from 10-Q Q2 FY2013 and 10-K FY2012 data

checklist.question: Does the filing chronology show irregularities (late filings/NT forms, amendments, unusual 8-K frequency)?

evidence.quote: Extended sequence of SEC comment-letter correspondence (UPLOAD/CORRESP filings) in mid-2010: 2010-06-23, 2010-06-30, 2010-07-08, 2010-07-22, 2010-07-23, 2010-07-29, 2010-08-04, 2010-08-17, 2010-08-25
A second round of SEC correspondence shortly before the cutoff: UPLOAD 2013-01-29, CORRESP 2013-02-01, CORRESP 2013-02-22, UPLOAD 2013-03-01, preceding the 10-K filed 2013-04-16

checklist.question: Is the provided data sufficient in coverage and length for the above assessments?

evidence.quote: Quarterly and annual data available continuously from FY2009 (period ending 2010-01-30) through Q2 FY2013 (period ending 2013-08-03), across Assets, Revenues, NetIncomeLoss, cash flow statement line items, and receivable/allowance detail

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Potential premature/aggressive revenue recognition or extended customer credit terms causing receivables to build faster than sales, combined with a shrinking bad-debt allowance ratio that would understate the contra-asset and overstate net receivables and income.

rationale_evidence.quote: AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=58,597,844.50 (2012-01-28) -> 70,240,282.90 (2013-02-02), +19.9% while Revenues declined -1.4% over the same fiscal year (384,665,826.57 -> 379,393,095.40)
AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent=11,171,845.73 (2012-01-28) fell to 10,403,358.65 (2013-02-02) and 8,046,772.28 (2013-08-03) even as gross AR balances grew

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Reported net income for FY2011 was not corroborated by cash generation, consistent with earnings being propped up by non-cash accruals or working-capital build (receivables/inventory) rather than genuine cash-generating sales; the subsequent large CFO rebound in FY2012 suggests partial unwind of this build-up.

rationale_evidence.quote: NetIncomeLoss=10,272,124.08 (FY2011: 2011-01-30 to 2012-01-28) vs NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivities=286,622.74 for the same period
FY2012 NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivities=30,989,473.06 vs NetIncomeLoss=5,958,290.89, a reversal following the prior year's near-zero operating cash flow

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Reduction in bad-debt reserve as a percentage of receivables (from ~19% to ~14%) could reflect under-provisioning for doubtful accounts, artificially reducing bad-debt expense and boosting reported net income during a period of receivables growth outpacing revenue.

rationale_evidence.quote: Allowance-to-AR ratio fell from ~19.1% (2012-01-28) to ~14.2% (2013-08-03) despite AR growth
rationale_evidence.location: derived from 10-Q/10-K receivable and allowance disclosures

top_signals: Receivables grew ~20% while revenue declined ~1.4% in FY2012 (AR=70,240,282.90 vs Revenues=379,393,095.40)
FY2011 net income ($10.27M) was almost entirely uncorroborated by operating cash flow ($0.29M), followed by an outsized FY2012 CFO rebound ($30.99M)
Allowance for doubtful accounts declined both in dollar terms and as a percentage of receivables (19.1%→14.2%) even as gross receivables grew
Extended SEC comment-letter correspondence in 2010 and again in early 2013 shortly before the cutoff filing
